summary: Raging Scarlet>Fire and a high‑energy second set
blurb: The second set erupts with an explosive Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam, blistering solos from Jerry and Keith, and a thunderous The Other One. Highlights include Samson & Delilah, Truckin' and a rousing U.S. Blues encore.
review: February 5, 1978 at the Uni Dome captured one of the spring ’78 tour’s fiercest nights. While the first set rolls along solidly with Brown Eyed Women and Deal, it’s the second set that cements this show in Deadhead lore – a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain stretch that reviewers call “on fire” and “raging,” followed by an extended The Other One where Keith Godchaux’s piano drives the band into “balls‑out” territory. Samson & Delilah adds a bluesy punch, Truckin' rolls straight into a tight drum break before the band launches into Wharf Rat and Around & Around, closing with a high‑octane U.S. Blues that leaves the crowd buzzing. Jerry Garcia’s guitar is in top form throughout, delivering snarling leads on Bertha and ferocious improvisations on the second‑set jams; Phil Lesh provides a solid, groove‑anchoring bass line, especially notable at The Other One’s intro. Bob Weir’s rhythm work stays crisp despite an early mic glitch that turned into a mini‑Zappa moment, while Keith’s piano is front‑and‑center, weaving through the jams and adding extra texture on Wharf Rat. Drummers Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann keep the pulse tight, with Bill’s double‑time feel on Scarlet Begonias giving the set an extra surge of excitement. The show shines brightest in the second half, offering a quintessential ’78 experience for anyone looking to hear the band at full tilt.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are marked positive, repeatedly using phrases like “blistering,” “raging,” “on fire,” and “legendary.” Multiple sources highlight the same standout moments – Scarlet>Fire, The Other One, Samson & Delilah – indicating strong consensus on performance excellence. No negative comments about playing quality appear, so a robust positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with crisp mix, minimal drop‑outs and the most detailed representation of the second‑set jams despite a few mic hiccups.

key_highlights: Explosive Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam that defines the night
Extended The Other One with Keith’s piano driving a “balls‑out” improvisation
Samson & Delilah adds blues power before the marathon second set
Tight, double‑time drumming on Scarlet and powerful U.S. Blues encore

band_performance.Jerry: Raging, on‑fire solos throughout; snarling tone on Bertha and blistering leads on Scarlet>Fire and The Other One.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with a standout intro to The Other One that locks the rhythm section in place.
band_performance.Bob: Steady rhythm work, vocal shouts, and a quirky mini‑Zappa moment despite an early mic glitch; keeps the groove tight.
band_performance.Keith: Dominant piano on The Other One and Scarlet>Fire, described as “balls‑out” and a key driver of the show’s intensity; extra weaving on Wharf Rat adds texture.
band_performance.Billy: Tight drumming with brief double‑time feel on Scarlet Begonias and supportive fills that propel the jams.
